title: Structured Outputs
learning_outcomes: Explain JSON Schema constraints in token logits.

content_sections.title: Guided Decoding Constraints
content_sections.content: To guarantee that LLM outputs conform to structured formats (such as JSON or YAML), inference engines apply **guided decoding**. At each generation step, a parser checks which vocabulary tokens are valid next characters under the target schema (e.g. after a JSON key name, the next token must be `:`). Invalid tokens have their logits shifted to $-\infty$, preventing them from being sampled.

content_sections.title: Logit Bias Equations
content_sections.content:
Logit bias modifications shift the probability distribution before the softmax layer:

$$\tilde{z}_i = z_i + \text{bias}_i$$

where $\text{bias}_i$ is set to a large negative number (e.g. $-10^9$) for invalid indices under the state constraints. This forces the model to select only from the subset of vocabulary tokens that conform to the target schema.
